title: Oh, Blest the House

W: 6.Then here will I and mine to-day
W: A solemn covenant make, and say,--

metadata.composer: 'Wo Gott Zum Haus' from Klug's Geistliche Lieder, 1533 "Evangelical Lutheran Hymn-Book", 1931
metadata.lyricist: Christoph C. L. von Pfeil, 1735. Translated by Catherine Winkworth, 1863, alt
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Christoph C. L. von Pfeil, 1735. Translated by Catherine Winkworth, 1863, alt.
Music: 'Wo Gott Zum Haus' from Klug's Geistliche Lieder, 1533.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: Oh blest the house, whate'er befall, Where Jesus Christ is All in All; Yea, if He were not dwelling there, How poor and dark and void it were!

2.raw_text: Oh blest that house where faith ye find, And all within have set their mind To trust their God and serve Him still, And do in all His holy will.

3.raw_text: Blest, where their prayers shall daily rise As fragrant incense to the skies, While in their lives the world is taught That forms without the heart are naught.

4.raw_text: Blest, where the busy hands fulfil Their proper task with ready skill, While through their different works ye see One spirit run of unity.

5.raw_text: Blest such a house, it prospers well, In peace and joy the parents dwell, And in their children's lot is shown How richly God can bless His own.
